join_order.build: sub(sic[], cik[]) — full arrays loaded into memory
join_order.notes: sub_sic (172KB) and sub_cik (344KB) fully loaded; O(1) lookup by adsh_code; sic BETWEEN 4000-4999 selectivity ~4%
tag_abstract (1MB) fully loaded; O(1) by tagver_code; abstract==0 ~95% pass

Bloom filter checked first: rejects ~99.2% of non-qualifying probes without touching eq_count_map. Bloom misses then probe eq_count_map (25MB, LLC-resident). FlatCountMap sized with cap_hint=700000 → 2,097,152 slots × 12 bytes = 25.2MB < 44MB LLC.

filters.notes: Zone maps skip entire 100K-row blocks; row-level check applied first
Most selective — applied before abstract and eq_count checks
Bloom filter pre-check then eq_count_map probe; applied last

predicate_order_per_num_row: 1. uom_code == usd_code (zone map at block level, then row check)
3. sub_sic[adsh_code] BETWEEN 4000 AND 4999 (most selective, array O(1))
5. bloom_filter check: 7 hashes into 2MB bitset — reject if any bit is 0

data_structures.sub_sic_array: flat_array(86135 × int16_t) — direct indexed by adsh_code
data_structures.sub_cik_array: flat_array(86135 × int32_t) — direct indexed by adsh_code
data_structures.tag_abstract_array: flat_array(1070662 × int8_t) — direct indexed by tagver_code
data_structures.bloom_filter: bitset(16777216 bits = 2MB); 7 independent hash functions; built from eq_count_map keys during pre-scan; used to reject non-qualifying num rows before eq_count_map probe
data_structures.eq_count_map: FlatCountMap with cap_hint=700000 → 2,097,152 slots × 12 bytes = 25.2MB (fits in 44MB LLC); key=((uint64_t)(uint32_t)adsh_code<<32)|(uint32_t)tagver_code; value=uint32_t count of EQ pre rows
data_structures.agg_map_per_thread: unordered_map<uint64_t, AggState> per thread; AggState = {double sum, int64_t cnt, vector<int32_t> ciks_sorted}; reserve(2048) per thread; ciks_sorted kept in sorted order for O(log n) dedup insert during scan
data_structures.aggregation: thread-local hash maps merged via parallel binary-tree reduction (6 rounds for 64 threads)

build_eq_count_map_and_bloom.strategy: parallel scan into thread-local key vectors; single-threaded insert into FlatCountMap; then bloom filter construction from populated map slots
build_eq_count_map_and_bloom.per_thread: vector<uint64_t> local_keys; push key when pre_stmt_code[i]==eq_code; key=((uint64_t)(uint32_t)pre_adsh_code[i]<<32)|(uint32_t)pre_tagver_code[i]; skip if key==UINT64_MAX
build_eq_count_map_and_bloom.merge_into_map: single-threaded loop over thread vectors calling eq_count_map.increment(k); estimated ~1.06M insertions into 2M-slot table
build_eq_count_map_and_bloom.bloom_build: after map is built, iterate over all occupied slots in eq_count_map and set bits in bloom_filter for each key using 7 hash functions; bloom_filter = vector<uint64_t>(262144) = 2MB = 16M bits

accumulators: ciks_sorted: vector<int32_t> kept sorted; insert via lower_bound+check to avoid duplicates
aggregation.merge_strategy: parallel binary-tree reduction: in each of 6 rounds, threads with stride s merge thread[i] into thread[i-s] in parallel (s=1,2,4,8,16,32); final result in thread_maps[0]. For each merged entry: sum+=src.sum; cnt+=src.cnt; merge ciks_sorted vectors using std::merge+std::unique inplace.
aggregation.post_having_decode: iterate global_agg[0], decode tlabel, re-aggregate by (sic,tlabel) string key, then HAVING+sort+limit

parallelism.merge_strategy: parallel binary-tree reduction (6 rounds for 64 threads, O(log n) depth)
parallelism.notes: 64 cores; merge_aggregates phase was ~150ms single-threaded — tree reduction brings this to ~6 parallel rounds of ~25ms peak = ~15ms

indexes.usage: skip block b if zm[b].min_uom > usd_code || zm[b].max_uom < usd_code; struct ZoneMap { int8_t min_uom, max_uom; int32_t min_ddate, max_ddate; } sizeof=12 (2-byte pad)

output.tlabel_decode: after primary aggregation merge, decode tlabel for each group from tlabel_offsets+tlabel_data by tagver_code; re-aggregate by (sic, tlabel) string to merge groups sharing the same tlabel; then HAVING cik_count>=2, sort, limit 500

optimization_notes: Three root causes diagnosed. (1) Category B: eq_count_map was allocated with FlatCountMap(2200000), which resolves to 8,388,608 slots × 12 bytes = 100.6MB — 2.3× the 44MB LLC, causing every main_scan probe to miss LLC. Fix: FlatCountMap(700000) → 2,097,152 slots × 25.2MB, LLC-resident; 1.06M entries at 50.6% load factor is well within open-addressing limits. (2) Category C: 89% of probes to eq_count_map were from non-qualifying rows (those that pass sic+abstract but not the eq_set). A 2MB bloom filter (16M bits, 7 hash functions, ~0.78% FPR) rejects ~99.2% of these non-qualifying probes before touching the hash table, eliminating wasted LLC-miss memory traffic. (3) Category D: merge_aggregates was fully single-threaded and consumed ~150ms (the largest single phase, invisible because it had no GENDB_PHASE timer). Fix: parallel binary-tree reduction — 6 rounds where active threads merge in pairs, cutting the merge to O(log 64) depth with each round fully parallelized. CIK tracking changed from unordered_set<int32_t> (expensive heap allocation per group) to sorted vector<int32_t> (insert via lower_bound dedup, merge via std::merge+unique), dramatically reducing allocation overhead and making cik-set merges branchless and cache-friendly.
